showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

bitly_url.description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

create_datetime.description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

embded_url.description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

featured_tags.description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

import_datetime.description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

rating.description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

slug.description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

source.description: The page on which this GIF was found

source_post_url.description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

source_tld.description: The top level domain of the source URL.

tags.description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

trending_datetime.description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

update_datetime.description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

url.description: The unique URL for this GIF

username.description: The username this GIF is attached to, if applicable

frames.description: The number of frames in this GIF.

height.description: The height of this GIF in pixels.

mp4.description: The URL for this GIF in .MP4 format.

mp4_size.description: The size in bytes of the .MP4 file corresponding to this GIF.

size.description: The size of this GIF in bytes.

url.description: The publicly-accessible direct URL for this GIF.

webp.description: The URL for this GIF in .webp format.

webp_size.description: The size in bytes of the .webp file corresponding to this GIF.

width.description: The width of this GIF in pixels.

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: The Meta Object contains basic information regarding the request, whether it was successful, and the response given by the API.  Check `responses` to see a description of types of response codes the API might give you under different cirumstances.

response_id.description: A unique ID paired with this response from the API.

Pagination.title: Pagination
Pagination.description: The Pagination Object contains information relating to the number of total results available as well as the number of results fetched and their relative positions.

User.title: User
User.description: The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

avatar_url.description: The URL for this user's avatar image.

banner_url.description: The URL for the banner image that appears atop this user's profile page.

display_name.description: The display name associated with this user (contains formatting the base username might not).

profile_url.description: The URL for this user's profile.

twitter.description: The Twitter username associated with this user, if applicable.

username.description: The username associated with this user.

Images.title: Images
Images.description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Type.title: Type
Type.description: Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

Nodes.Text: Giphy API
Official Giphy Documentation: [https://developers.giphy.com/docs/](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/)
Requirements

The SDK relies on **Node.js** and **npm** (to resolve dependencies). It also requires **Typescript version >=4.1**. You can download and install Node.js and [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/) from [the official Node.js website](https://nodejs.org/en/download/).
> **NOTE:** npm is installed by default when Node.js is installed.
Verify Successful Installation
Run the following commands in the command prompt or shell of your choice to check if Node.js and npm are successfully installed:
* Node.js: `node --version`
* npm: `npm --version`
Install Dependencies
- To resolve all dependencies, go to the **SDK root directory** and run the following command with npm:

```bash
npm install
```
- This will install all dependencies in the **node_modules** folder.
The following section explains how to use the generated library in a new project.
- Open an IDE/text editor for JavaScript like Visual Studio Code. The basic workflow presented here is also applicable if you prefer using a different editor or IDE.
- Click on **File** and select **Open Folder**. Select an empty folder of your project, the folder will become visible in the sidebar on the left.
- To initialize the Node project, click on **Terminal** and select **New Terminal**. Execute the following command in the terminal:

```bash
npm init --y
```

- The created project manages its dependencies using its `package.json` file. In order to add a dependency on the *Giphy APILib* client library, double click on the ``` package.json ``` file in the bar on the left and add the dependency to the package in it.
- To install the package in the project, run the following command in the terminal:

```bash
npm install
```

Steps.Title: Add Dependencies to the Client Library

Nodes.Text: The following parameters are configurable for the API Client:
The API client can be initialized as follows:
Code-Based Client Initialization
Configuration-Based Client Initialization
See the [Configuration-Based Client Initialization]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/Configuration-Based%20Client%20Initialization) section for details.
Environment-Based Client Initialization
See the [Environment-Based Client Initialization]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/Environment-Based%20Client%20Initialization) section for details.

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`api_key (Custom Query Parameter)`]($h/__auth_CustomQueryAuthentication)
Documentation for accessing and setting credentials for api_key.
**Note:** Auth credentials can be set using `customQueryAuthenticationCredentials` object in the client.
You must provide credentials in the client as shown in the following code snippet.

* [Gifs]($e/gifs)
* [Stickers]($e/stickers)
The instance of the `GifsApi` class can be created using the API Client.
const gifsApi = new GifsApi(client);

Nodes.Title: Get Gifs by Id
Nodes.Name: getGifsById
Nodes.Description: A multiget version of the get GIF by ID endpoint.
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `result` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Response).

MethodSignature.Text:
async getGifsById(
  ids?: string,
  requestOptions?: RequestOptions
): Promise<ApiResponse<GifsResponse>>

Parameters.Name: ids
Parameters.Description: Filters results by specified GIF IDs, separated by commas.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: Your request was formatted incorrectly or missing required parameters.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: You weren't authorized to make your request; most likely this indicates an issue with your API Key.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: The particular GIF you are requesting was not found. This occurs, for example, if you request a GIF by an id that does not exist.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: Your API Key is making too many requests. Read about [requesting a Production Key](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/#access) to upgrade your API Key rate limits.

ids.description: Filters results by specified GIF IDs, separated by commas.

Response.Description: This endpoint returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `result` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Response).

Response.Name: response400
Response.Description: Your request was formatted incorrectly or missing required parameters.

Response.Name: response403

Response.Name: response429
Response.Description: Your API Key is making too many requests. Read about [requesting a Production Key](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/#access) to upgrade your API Key rate limits.

Nodes.Title: Random Gif
Nodes.Name: randomGif
Nodes.Description: Returns a random GIF, limited by tag. Excluding the tag parameter will return a random GIF from the GIPHY catalog.
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `result` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsRandomResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Random%20Response).

Nodes.Title: Search Gifs
Nodes.Name: searchGifs
Nodes.Description: Search all GIPHY GIFs for a word or phrase. Punctuation will be stripped and ignored.  Use a plus or url encode for phrases. Example paul+rudd, ryan+gosling or american+psycho.
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `result` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsSearchResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Search%20Response).

MethodSignature.Text:
async searchGifs(
  q: string,
  limit?: number,
  offset?: number,
  rating?: string,
  lang?: string,
  requestOptions?: RequestOptions
): Promise<ApiResponse<GifsSearchResponse>>

Parameters.Name: limit
Parameters.Description:
The maximum number of records to return.

**Default**: `25`

Parameters.Name: lang
Parameters.Description: Specify default language for regional content; use a 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: Your request was formatted incorrectly or missing required parameters.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: You weren't authorized to make your request; most likely this indicates an issue with your API Key.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: The particular GIF you are requesting was not found. This occurs, for example, if you request a GIF by an id that does not exist.

Errors.Name: ApiError
Errors.Description: Your API Key is making too many requests. Read about [requesting a Production Key](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/#access) to upgrade your API Key rate limits.

limit.description: The maximum number of records to return.; Default: 25

lang.description: Specify default language for regional content; use a 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code.

UsageExample.HttpCallTemplate: {"method":"GET","url":"https://api.giphy.com/v1/gifs/search{% capture inline_query_capture_876234 %}{% if auth["api_key"]["api_key"] != nil %}&api_key={% if auth["api_key"]["api_key"] != nil %}{{ auth["api_key"]["api_key"] | url_encode }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% endif %}&q={% if call["args"]["q"] != nil %}{{ call["args"]["q"] | url_encode }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'limit' %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'limit' %}&limit={% if call["args"]["limit"] != nil %}{{ call["args"]["limit"] }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'offset' %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'offset' %}&offset={% if call["args"]["offset"] != nil %}{{ call["args"]["offset"] }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'rating' %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'rating' %}&rating={% if call["args"]["rating"] != nil %}{{ call["args"]["rating"] | url_encode }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'lang' %}{% if call["args"] hasKey 'lang' %}&lang={% if call["args"]["lang"] != nil %}{{ call["args"]["lang"] | url_encode }}{% else %}null{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endcapture %}{% if inline_query_capture_876234.size > 0 %}?{% endif %}{{ inline_query_capture_876234 | remove_first:'&' }}","headers":{% capture header_capture_9023987234 %},"Accept":"application/json"{% endcapture %}{{ '{' }}{{ header_capture_9023987234 | remove_first:',' }}},"body":{{ '{' }}"type":"none"}}

Response.Name: response200
Response.Description:
Search results

This endpoint returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `result` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsSearchResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Search%20Response).

Fields.Name: bitlyUrl
Fields.Description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

Fields.Name: contentUrl
Fields.Description: Currently unused

Fields.Name: createDatetime
Fields.Description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

Fields.Name: embdedUrl
Fields.Description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

Fields.Name: featuredTags
Fields.Description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

Fields.Name: id
Fields.Description: This GIF's unique ID

Fields.Name: images
Fields.Description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Fields.Name: importDatetime
Fields.Description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

Fields.Name: rating
Fields.Description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

Fields.Name: slug
Fields.Description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

Fields.Name: source
Fields.Description: The page on which this GIF was found

Fields.Name: sourcePostUrl
Fields.Description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

Fields.Name: sourceTld
Fields.Description: The top level domain of the source URL.

Fields.Name: tags
Fields.Description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

Fields.Name: trendingDatetime
Fields.Description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

Fields.Name: type
Fields.Description:
Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

**Default**: `Type.Gif`

Fields.Name: updateDatetime
Fields.Description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

Fields.Name: url
Fields.Description: The unique URL for this GIF

Fields.Name: user
Fields.Description: The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

Fields.Name: username
Fields.Description: The username this GIF is attached to, if applicable

Nodes.Title: Gif
Nodes.Name: Gif
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type unknown.*

Fields.Name: frames
Fields.Description: The number of frames in this GIF.

Fields.Name: height
Fields.Description: The height of this GIF in pixels.

Fields.Name: mp4
Fields.Description: The URL for this GIF in .MP4 format.

Fields.Name: mp4Size
Fields.Description: The size in bytes of the .MP4 file corresponding to this GIF.

Fields.Name: size
Fields.Description: The size of this GIF in bytes.

Fields.Name: url
Fields.Description: The publicly-accessible direct URL for this GIF.

Fields.Name: webp
Fields.Description: The URL for this GIF in .webp format.

Fields.Name: webpSize
Fields.Description: The size in bytes of the .webp file corresponding to this GIF.

Fields.Name: width
Fields.Description: The width of this GIF in pixels.

Nodes.Title: Meta
Nodes.Name: Meta
Nodes.Description:
The Meta Object contains basic information regarding the request, whether it was successful, and the response given by the API.  Check `responses` to see a description of types of response codes the API might give you under different cirumstances.

*This model accepts additional fields of type unknown.*

Fields.Name: count
Fields.Description: Total number of items returned.

Fields.Name: offset
Fields.Description: Position in pagination.

Fields.Name: totalCount
Fields.Description: Total number of items available.

Nodes.Title: Pagination
Nodes.Name: Pagination
Nodes.Description:
The Pagination Object contains information relating to the number of total results available as well as the number of results fetched and their relative positions.

*This model accepts additional fields of type unknown.*

Fields.Name: avatarUrl
Fields.Description: The URL for this user's avatar image.

Fields.Name: bannerUrl
Fields.Description: The URL for the banner image that appears atop this user's profile page.

Fields.Name: displayName
Fields.Description: The display name associated with this user (contains formatting the base username might not).

Fields.Name: profileUrl
Fields.Description: The URL for this user's profile.

Fields.Name: twitter
Fields.Description: The Twitter username associated with this user, if applicable.

Fields.Name: username
Fields.Description: The username associated with this user.

Nodes.Title: User
Nodes.Name: User
Nodes.Description:
The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

Nodes.Title: Type

Nodes.Description: Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

Example.Text:
import { Type } from 'giphy-apilib';

const type = Type.Gif;

Nodes.Text: Represents the HTTP client configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data: Custom http agent to be used when performing http requests.
Custom https agent to be used when performing http requests.
Proxy server configurations to route both HTTP and HTTPS requests through a proxy.

Nodes.Text: Represents the retry configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data:
Whether to retry on request timeout.
*Default*: `true`

Interval before next retry. Used in calculation of wait time for next request in case of failure.
*Default*: `1`

Overall wait time for the requests getting retried.
*Default*: `0`

Used in calculation of wait time for next request in case of failure.
*Default*: `2`

Nodes.Text: Represents the proxy server configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data: The port to connect to the proxy server.

Nodes.Text: ProxyAuth
Client Initialization with Proxy Configuration
To configure the SDK to use a proxy server, initialize the proxy configuration during client setup as shown in the Usage Example.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example

Nodes.Text: The SDK client can be initialized directly from a JSON configuration file using the `fromJsonConfig` static method available on the `Client` class.` This allows you to store and load configuration settings from JSON files, making it easier to organize and manage different configurations across environments.
Node.js Environment
In Node.js environments, you can use the `fs` module to read the configuration file from the file system.
Browser Environment
In browser environments, you can use the `import` function to load the configuration file.
Example Configuration File

The SDK client can be initialized directly from environment variables using the `fromEnvironment` static method available on the `Client` class. This allows you to configure the SDK through environment variables, making it easier to manage different settings across different environments.
Node.js Environment
In Node.js environments, you can use the `dotenv` package to load environment variables from a `.env` file and pass `process.env` to the configuration.
The `fromEnvironment` method accepts an optional parameter for environment variables. If not provided, it defaults to `process.env`.
Browser Environment

In browser environments, you can pass configuration values directly as key-value pairs to the `fromEnvironment` method.  Since `process.env` is not available in browsers, environment variables must be passed as an object.
Example .env File

# Basic Configuration
TIMEOUT=30000
ENVIRONMENT=production

# Authentication Credentials
API_KEY=api_key

# Retry Configuration
MAX_NUMBER_OF_RETRIES=3
RETRY_ON_TIMEOUT=true
RETRY_INTERVAL=10
MAX_RETRY_WAIT_TIME=120
RETRY_BACKOFF_FACTOR=2
HTTP_STATUS_CODES_TO_RETRY=408,413,429,500,502
HTTP_METHODS_TO_RETRY=GET,PUT,DELETE

# Proxy Settings
PROXY_ADDRESS=http://localhost:3000
PROXY_PORT=8080
PROXY_AUTH_USERNAME=username
PROXY_AUTH_PASSWORD=password

# Logging
LOG_LEVEL=info
MASK_SENSITIVE_HEADERS=true

# Request Logging
REQUEST_LOG_BODY=true
REQUEST_LOG_HEADERS=true
REQUEST_INCLUDE_QUERY_IN_PATH=true
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_INCLUDE=Content-Type,X-Request-ID
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_EXCLUDE=Authorization
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_WHITELIST=X-Request-ID

# Response Logging
RESPONSE_LOG_BODY=true
RESPONSE_LOG_HEADERS=true
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_INCLUDE=Content-Type,X-Correlation-ID,Date,Server
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_EXCLUDE=Set-Cookie,Authorization,X-API-Key
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_WHITELIST=X-Correlation-ID
Represents the logging configurations for API calls.
Properties
Represents the request logging configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data:
Array of headers which values are non-senstive to display in logging.
*Default*: `[]`

Nodes.Text: Represents the request logging configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data:
Response Headers to include in logging
*Default*: `[]`

Response Headers to exclude in logging
*Default*: `[]`

Array of headers which values are non-senstive to display in logging.
*Default*: `[]`

Nodes.Text: An interface for custom logger implementation.
Logger Methods

Data: Logs a message with a specified log level and additional parameters.

1. level: The log level of the message.
2. message: The message template to log.
3. params: The parameters to include in the log message.
log(level: LogLevel, message: string, params: Record<string, any>): void

Nodes.Text: Usage Example
Custom LoggerInterface implementation

In order to provide custom logger, any implementation of the `LoggerInterface` can be used so that you can override the `log` behavior and provide its instance directly in the SDK client initialization. The following example exports a `WinstonLogger` class that implements the `LoggerInterface`'s `log()` function.
Client Initialization with Custom Logger
The following example uses the above `WinstonLogger` implementation of `LoggerInterface` for GiphyApi Client initialization.
Represents an HTTP request.
Properties

Data: ```GET | DELETE | HEAD | OPTIONS | POST | PUT | PATCH | LINK | UNLINK```
Request URL including the query part.

Nodes.Text: HttpRequestBody
Represents the HTTP request body, which can be of different types.
HttpRequestTextBody
HttpRequestUrlEncodedFormBody
HttpRequestMultipartFormBody
HttpRequestStreamBody
Represents the result of an API call, including response metadata and the returned data of type `T`.
Properties

Data: Original request that resulted in this response.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example

Nodes.Text:
Thrown when the HTTP status code is not okay.

The ApiError extends the ApiResponse interface, so all ApiResponse properties are available.
Properties

Data: Original request that resulted in this response.

Nodes.Text: This developer portal is powered by **APIMatic**. The same platform that generated this experience can turn any OpenAPI specification into a complete developer portal — interactive documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, live API testing, and AI-assisted onboarding — in minutes.

Context Plugins load your full API context — docs, SDKs, code samples, and setup instructions — into AI coding environments like Claude Code and Cursor, so developers integrate your API through natural language instead of a wall of reference docs. A built-in portal copilot is available too.

API Reference documentation with language‑specific docs and SDK guides — including idiomatic, ready-to-run code samples complete with imports across all seven SDK languages. Developers can explore your API structure before writing any code.
Built-in testing interface supporting all major authentication methods. Developers can make real API calls, see actual responses, and validate their integration approach.
Production-ready client libraries in Java, Python, TypeScript, C#, PHP, Ruby, and Go. Each SDK includes comprehensive documentation, code samples, and follows language-specific best practices.
Step-by-step onboarding flows to help developers implement key API usecases faster.
Full control over colors, fonts, logos, and layout. Add custom documentation pages, modify navigation, and ensure the portal reflects your brand identity.
Bring your API to APIMatic and ship a developer portal like this one in minutes.
See how APIMatic turns your API specification into a branded, AI-ready developer portal.
Dive into the Advanced Features Guide to set up and customize your own portal.

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, custom URL slugs, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: **Context Plugins** let developers connect your API to the AI coding assistant they already use (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more) to search, ask questions, and generate working integration code grounded in your live specification.

The portal also includes a built-in **API Copilot** that answers questions from your API reference and product documentation, and an **AI Assist Menu** that sends any documentation page straight to your preferred AI tool as context with one click.
Automatic **LLMs.txt generation** and per-page Markdown exports make your documentation AI-accessible to tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude.
